verses.text: God speaks against Edom, who exalted himself as the eagle, his nest among the stars; 'thence will I bring thee down.' Pride toppled. No height of security can shield the proud from God.
God speaks against Edom, who exalted himself as the eagle, his nest among the stars; 'thence will I bring thee down.' Pride toppled. No height of security can shield the proud from God.
God speaks against Edom, who exalted himself as the eagle, his nest among the stars; 'thence will I bring thee down.' Pride toppled. No height of security can shield the proud from God.
God speaks against Edom, who exalted himself as the eagle, his nest among the stars; 'thence will I bring thee down.' Pride toppled. No height of security can shield the proud from God.
Thieves would leave some, but Esau's hidden things are wholly ransacked; his allies have deceived him. Stripped bare. Those who trust in confederates find them treacherous.
Thieves would leave some, but Esau's hidden things are wholly ransacked; his allies have deceived him. Stripped bare. Those who trust in confederates find them treacherous.
Thieves would leave some, but Esau's hidden things are wholly ransacked; his allies have deceived him. Stripped bare. Those who trust in confederates find them treacherous.
God will destroy the wise men of Edom and dismay her mighty; her boasted wisdom and strength fail. Vain reliance. Human cunning and power are nothing before God.
God will destroy the wise men of Edom and dismay her mighty; her boasted wisdom and strength fail. Vain reliance. Human cunning and power are nothing before God.
For violence against 'thy brother Jacob,' shame shall cover Edom, who stood aside in the day of his calamity. Brotherly betrayal. To stand by while a brother suffers is itself a sin.
For violence against 'thy brother Jacob,' shame shall cover Edom, who stood aside in the day of his calamity. Brotherly betrayal. To stand by while a brother suffers is itself a sin.
He should not have gloated over Judah's day nor cut off her fugitives in the crossway. Cruelty in another's grief. God marks every malice shown in a neighbour's distress.
He should not have gloated over Judah's day nor cut off her fugitives in the crossway. Cruelty in another's grief. God marks every malice shown in a neighbour's distress.
He should not have gloated over Judah's day nor cut off her fugitives in the crossway. Cruelty in another's grief. God marks every malice shown in a neighbour's distress.
'The day of the LORD is near upon all the heathen'; as Edom did, so shall be done to him. Just recompense. God repays the nations in the very coin of their cruelty.
'The day of the LORD is near upon all the heathen'; as Edom did, so shall be done to him. Just recompense. God repays the nations in the very coin of their cruelty.
But on Mount Zion shall be 'deliverance, and there shall be holiness'; Jacob shall be a fire, and Esau stubble. Salvation and judgment. The same day saves God's people and consumes their foes.
But on Mount Zion shall be 'deliverance, and there shall be holiness'; Jacob shall be a fire, and Esau stubble. Salvation and judgment. The same day saves God's people and consumes their foes.
The land shall be repossessed, saviours shall come up on Zion to judge Esau, 'and the kingdom shall be the LORD's.' The end of all. History closes with the reign of God over all.
